name: Yama's Fish Market
tagline: The local plate lunch and poke counter that office workers plan their whole week around.
mustOrder: Ahi poke — classic shoyu or spicy, always fresh-cut
Kalua pig plate — smoky pulled pork with cabbage, rice, and mac salad
Laulau plate — steamed pork and taro leaf with two scoops rice
parking: Small lot that fills up fast at lunch. Try street parking on Young St or Isenberg.
talkStory: The Yamashiro family has been running this Moiliili plate lunch and poke counter for decades, and office workers from UH to Ala Moana plan their week around it. Locals argue endlessly whether Yama's or Ono Seafood has better ahi poke — both are great, different styles. The kalua pig plate is enormous and the poke is always fresh-cut. Lunch rush is 11:30-1pm with lines out the door. Go at 11am or after 1:30. They sell poke by the pound — perfect beach day move.
